items.title: A win for practices: No Surprises Act dispute fee falls to $15 - Medical Economics
items.reason: This item directly addresses a concrete change to the Independent Dispute Resolution (IDR) process under the No Surprises Act—specifically a reduction in dispute filing fees—which is a substantive rule modification affecting healthcare providers and revenue-cycle operations.
items.summary: The dispute filing fee for the No Surprises Act's Independent Dispute Resolution (IDR) process has been reduced to $15, according to Medical Economics. This represents a decrease from previously established fee levels and constitutes a favorable change for medical practices engaged in payment disputes under the Act. The lower IDR filing fee reduces the financial barrier for providers to challenge insurance payment decisions, potentially increasing participation in the dispute resolution process. This modification directly impacts revenue-cycle teams' cost-benefit analysis when deciding whether to pursue IDR claims for out-of-network claim disputes.

also.title: Congressional Budget Office contends provider wins undermining No Surprises Act - Radiology Business
